---
title: "BosphorusBytes"
sidebar: false
---

# Istanbul Airbnb Analysis: How Short-Term Rentals Are Reshaping the City

---

## Team Members

| Name | Student ID |
|------|-----------|
| [Mevl??t G??rg??l??](https://github.com/) | 2210469027 |
| [S??la Nur G??ney](https://github.com/) | 2210469060 |
| [Aleyna Asiye Ko??](https://github.com/) | 2210469076 |
| [Melike Ebrar ??ahinbay](https://github.com/) | 2210469077 |

**GitHub:** [emu-hacettepe-analytics/BosphorusBytes](https://github.com/emu-hacettepe-analytics/emu430-fall2024-team-bosphorusbytes-3)

---

## Project Description

Istanbul is one of the world's most-visited cities, and the rise of Airbnb has fundamentally changed its short-term rental landscape. This project analyses the **Istanbul Airbnb dataset** from [Inside Airbnb](http://insideairbnb.com) to uncover how short-term rentals are distributed across the city, what drives listing prices, how the market evolved over time (including through COVID-19), and what tourism pressure individual neighbourhoods face.

---

## Key Takeaways

- ??????? **Listings are heavily concentrated in tourist cores:** Beyo??lu, ??i??li, and Fatih together account for over 45% of all Istanbul listings.

- ???? **Location and room type are the strongest price predictors:** Entire-home listings in central districts cost 2???3?? more than private rooms in peripheral areas.

- ???? **COVID-19 caused a near-total booking collapse:** Monthly review counts fell ~85% between February and May 2020, with full recovery not until mid-2022.

- ???? **Professional hosts dominate supply:** The top 5% of hosts by listing count control over 30% of total listings.

- ???? **Guests talk about location above all else:** The words *location*, *clean*, and *walk* dominate reviews in central districts.

---

*Go to [Data](data.qmd) to explore the dataset, or [Analysis](analysis.qmd) for findings and visualisations.*
